info.title: Bitwarden Public API
info.description:
This schema documents the endpoints available to the Public API, which provides
organizations tools for managing members, collections, groups, event logs, and policies.
If you are looking for the Vault Management API, refer instead to
[this document](https://bitwarden.com/help/vault-management-api/).

**Note:** your authorization must match the server you have selected.

license.name: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

get.description:
Retrieves the details of an existing collection. You need only supply the unique collection identifier
that was returned upon collection creation.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifier of the collection to be retrieved.

put.description:
Updates the specified collection object. If a property is not provided,
the value of the existing property will be reset.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifier of the collection to be updated.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a collection. This cannot be undone.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifier of the collection to be deleted.

get.description:
Returns a list of your organization's collections.
Collection objects listed in this call do not include information about their associated groups.

Returns a filtered list of your organization's event logs, paged by a continuation token.
If no filters are provided, it will return the last 30 days of event for the organization.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: The start date. Must be less than the end date.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: The end date. Must be greater than the start date.

parameters.name: actingUserId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the user that performed the event.

parameters.name: itemId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the related item that the event describes.

parameters.name: secretId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the related secret that the event describes.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the related project that the event describes.

parameters.name: continuationToken
parameters.description: A cursor for use in pagination.

get.description:
Retrieves the details of an existing group. You need only supply the unique group identifier
that was returned upon group creation.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifier of the group to be retrieved.

put.description:
Updates the specified group object. If a property is not provided,
the value of the existing property will be reset.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifier of the group to be updated.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a group. This cannot be undone.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifier of the group to be deleted.

get.description:
Retrieves the unique identifiers for all members that are associated with this group. You need only
supply the unique group identifier that was returned upon group creation.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifier of the group to be retrieved.

put.description: Updates the specified group's member associations.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifier of the group to be updated.

get.description:
Returns a list of your organization's groups.
Group objects listed in this call include information about their associated collections.

Retrieves the details of an existing member of the organization. You need only supply the
unique member identifier that was returned upon member creation.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifier of the member to be retrieved.

put.description:
Updates the specified member object. If a property is not provided,
the value of the existing property will be reset.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifier of the member to be updated.

delete.description: Removes a member from the organization. This cannot be undone. The user account will still remain.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifier of the member to be removed.

get.description:
Retrieves the unique identifiers for all groups that are associated with this member. You need only
supply the unique member identifier that was returned upon member creation.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifier of the member to be retrieved.

put.description: Updates the specified member's group associations.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifier of the member to be updated.

get.description:
Returns a list of your organization's members.
Member objects listed in this call include information about their associated collections.

post.description: Creates a new member object by inviting a user to the organization.
Re-sends the invitation email to an organization member.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifier of the member to re-invite.

post.summary: Revoke a member's access to an organization.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the member to be revoked.

post.description: Restores a previously revoked member of the organization.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifier of the member to be restored.

get.summary: Retrieves the subscription details for the current organization.

put.summary: Update the organization's current subscription for Password Manager and/or Secrets Manager.

requestBody.description: The request model containing the updated subscription information.

post.description: Import members and groups from an external system.

get.description: Retrieves the details of a policy.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: The type of policy to be retrieved.

put.description:
Updates the specified policy. If a property is not provided,
the value of the existing property will be reset.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: The type of policy to be updated.

get.description: Returns a list of your organization's policies.

readOnly.description: When true, the read only permission will not allow the user or group to make changes to items.

hidePasswords.description:
When true, the hide passwords permission will not allow the user or group to view passwords.
This prevents easy copy-and-paste of hidden items, however it may not completely prevent user access.

manage.description:
When true, the manage permission allows a user to both edit the ciphers within a collection and edit the users/groups that are assigned to the collection.
This field will not affect behavior until your organization is using the latest collection enhancements (Releasing Q1, 2024)

readOnly.description: When true, the read only permission will not allow the user or group to make changes to items.

hidePasswords.description:
When true, the hide passwords permission will not allow the user or group to view passwords.
This prevents easy copy-and-paste of hidden items, however it may not completely prevent user access.

manage.description:
When true, the manage permission allows a user to both edit the ciphers within a collection and edit the users/groups that are assigned to the collection.
This field will not affect behavior until your organization is using the latest collection enhancements (Releasing Q1, 2024)

externalId.description: External identifier for reference or linking this collection to another system.

object.description: String representing the object's type. Objects of the same type share the same properties.

groups.description: The associated groups that this collection is assigned to.

object.description: String representing the object's type. Objects of the same type share the same properties.

data.description: An array containing the actual response elements, paginated by any request parameters.

externalId.description: External identifier for reference or linking this collection to another system.

groups.description: The associated groups that this collection is assigned to.

object.description: String representing the object's type. Objects of the same type share the same properties.

message.description: A human-readable message providing details about the error.

errors.description:
If multiple errors occurred, they are listed in dictionary. Errors related to a specific
request parameter will include a dictionary key describing that parameter.

object.description: String representing the object's type. Objects of the same type share the same properties.

itemId.description: The unique identifier of the related item that the event describes.

collectionId.description: The unique identifier of the related collection that the event describes.

groupId.description: The unique identifier of the related group that the event describes.

policyId.description: The unique identifier of the related policy that the event describes.

memberId.description: The unique identifier of the related member that the event describes.

actingUserId.description: The unique identifier of the user that performed the event.

installationId.description: The Unique identifier of the Installation that performed the event.

date.description: The date/timestamp when the event occurred.

ipAddress.description: The IP address of the acting user.

secretId.description: The unique identifier of the related secret that the event describes.

projectId.description: The unique identifier of the related project that the event describes.

serviceAccountId.description: The unique identifier of the related service account that the event describes.

object.description: String representing the object's type. Objects of the same type share the same properties.

data.description: An array containing the actual response elements, paginated by any request parameters.

continuationToken.description: A cursor for use in pagination.

externalId.description: External identifier for reference or linking this group to another system, such as a user directory.

collections.description: The associated collections that this group can access.

externalId.description: External identifier for reference or linking this group to another system, such as a user directory.

object.description: String representing the object's type. Objects of the same type share the same properties.

collections.description: The associated collections that this group can access.

object.description: String representing the object's type. Objects of the same type share the same properties.

externalId.description: External identifier for reference or linking this member to another system, such as a user directory.

collections.description: The associated collections that this member can access.

groups.description: Ids of the associated groups that this member will belong to

externalId.description: External identifier for reference or linking this member to another system, such as a user directory.

object.description: String representing the object's type. Objects of the same type share the same properties.

id.description: The member's unique identifier within the organization.

userId.description: The member's unique identifier across Bitwarden.

name.description: The member's name, set from their user account profile.

twoFactorEnabled.description: Returns `true` if the member has a two-step login method enabled on their user account.

collections.description: The associated collections that this member can access.

resetPasswordEnrolled.description: Returns `true` if the member has enrolled in Password Reset assistance within the organization

ssoExternalId.description: SSO external identifier for linking this member to an identity provider.

object.description: String representing the object's type. Objects of the same type share the same properties.

data.description: An array containing the actual response elements, paginated by any request parameters.

externalId.description: External identifier for reference or linking this member to another system, such as a user directory.

collections.description: The associated collections that this member can access.

groups.description: Ids of the associated groups that this member will belong to

externalId.description: External identifier for reference or linking this group to another system, such as a user directory.

memberExternalIds.description: The associated external ids for members in this group.

email.description: The member's email address. Required for non-deleted users.

externalId.description: External identifier for reference or linking this member to another system, such as a user directory.

deleted.description: Determines if this member should be removed from the organization during import.

overwriteExisting.description: Determines if the data in this request should overwrite or append to the existing organization data.

largeImport.description: Indicates an import of over 2000 users and/or groups is expected

OrganizationUserStatusType.description:
Represents the different stages of a member's lifecycle in an organization.
The Bit.Core.Entities.OrganizationUser object is populated differently depending on their Status.

PermissionsModel.description: Represents a member's custom permissions if the member has a Custom role.

enabled.description: Determines if this policy is enabled and enforced.

object.description: String representing the object's type. Objects of the same type share the same properties.
String representing the object's type. Objects of the same type share the same properties.

data.description: An array containing the actual response elements, paginated by any request parameters.

enabled.description: Determines if this policy is enabled and enforced.

groupIds.description: The associated group ids that this object can access.

memberIds.description: The associated member ids that have access to this object.

US_server.description: **Use this option if you've selected the US server**

EU_server.description: **Use this option if you've selected the EU server**
